item.title: Can JavaScript Escape a CSP Meta Tag Inside an Iframe? 摘要
item.description:
This research investigates whether JavaScript can bypass a Content Security Policy (CSP) meta tag within a sandboxed iframe, inspired by building a Claude Artifacts alternative. It finds that injecting a <meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy"> tag at the start of iframe content effectively enforces CSP, even if untrusted JavaScript attempts to manipulate it later, providing a method to apply CSP without needing a separate domain for file hosting.
标签: security, web, javascript, content-security-policy, sandboxing, iframes, web-development, browser-security, frontend, research

item.title: Roman moon, Greek moon 摘要
item.description:
The article explains the term 'perilune' used in the context of the Artemis II flight path, where it refers to the closest approach to the moon on the side opposite Earth. It discusses alternative terms like 'periselene', derived from Greek and Roman moon goddesses, and introduces general orbital terms such as 'periapsis' and 'apoapsis'. Specific examples include 'perigee'/'apogee' for Earth, 'perihelion'/'aphelion' for the Sun, and more obscure terms like 'perijove'/'apojove' for Jupiter and 'periareion'/'apoareion' for Mars, highlighting the linguistic origins and familiarity of these astronomical terms.
标签: astronomy, space, orbital mechanics, terminology, Artemis, moon, Greek mythology, Roman mythology, science, education

item.title: Apple Still Has Jessica Chastain’s ‘The Savant’ on Ice, Seven Months After It Was Set to Debut 摘要
item.description:
John Voorhees reports on Apple's April 2026 content updates for Apple TV and Apple Arcade, highlighting the continued indefinite delay of Jessica Chastain's political thriller 'The Savant', originally scheduled for September 2025 but postponed 'at a later date' due to alleged cowardice. The article compares Apple's vague scheduling to Trump's infamous 'in two weeks' promises, noting the show remains shelved seven months after its planned debut.
标签: Apple, Apple TV, Apple Arcade, streaming, content delay, Jessica Chastain, The Savant, entertainment, media, scheduling

item.title: Apple Releases iOS 18 Security Updates for iOS 26 Holdouts 摘要
item.description:
Apple has released iOS 18.7.7 security updates for devices capable of running iOS 26, addressing previous complaints about withholding patches. This move responds to serious security threats like DarkSword and Coruna. The author notes that while Apple supports staying a year behind on macOS, it doesn't on iOS, though the differences between iOS 18 and 26 feel minimal and not as drastic as macOS version changes.
标签: Apple, iOS, security, updates, mobile, operating systems, technology, software, patches, user experience

item.title: Premium: AI Isn't Too Big To Fail 摘要
item.description:
The article critiques the AI industry bubble, arguing that comparisons to past tech booms like the dot-com era or AWS are flawed and lack evidence. It highlights issues such as unproven profitability in AI inference, exaggerated data center construction claims (e.g., only 5GW of AI data centers are actually being built globally), and failures like the collapsed Poolside-CoreWeave deal. The author emphasizes that media and investors often avoid deep skepticism, perpetuating myths like 'AI is too big to fail,' while real-world signs—such as lack of buyer interest in OpenAI shares and layoffs at companies like Oracle—suggest underlying economic weaknesses. The piece calls for rigorous analysis over lazy tropes to prevent public misinformation and poor investment decisions.
标签: AI, bubble, economics, critique, data-centers, investment, skepticism, tech-industry, profitability, mythbusting

item.title: Quoting Greg Kroah-Hartman 摘要
item.description:
Linux kernel maintainer Greg Kroah-Hartman discusses the evolution of AI-generated security reports in open source projects. Initially, these reports were low-quality 'AI slop' that was obviously wrong and not concerning. However, about a month ago, there was a significant shift where AI-generated reports became high-quality and real, now being used effectively across all open source projects.
标签: AI, security, Linux, open-source, generative-ai, LLMs, AI-security-research, kernel, maintenance, reports

item.title: Quoting Daniel Stenberg 摘要
item.description:
Daniel Stenberg, lead developer of cURL, notes that AI's impact on open source security has shifted from generating low-quality content ("AI slop tsunami") to producing a high volume of security reports, many of which are valuable. He reports spending hours daily reviewing these reports, indicating the intensity and significance of this development in security practices.
标签: AI, security, open-source, curl, generative-ai, LLMs, AI-security-research, security-reports, Daniel-Stenberg

item.title: Quoting Willy Tarreau 摘要
item.description:
Willy Tarreau reports a dramatic increase in kernel security vulnerability reports, rising from 2-3 per week two years ago to 5-10 per day currently, with a significant spike attributed to AI-generated reports. This surge has led to duplicate findings and necessitated additional maintainer support to handle the volume.
标签: security, linux, kernel, vulnerability, AI, generative-ai, llms, maintenance, reports, open-source

item.title: Writing an LLM from scratch, part 32h -- Interventions: full fat float32 摘要
item.description:
The author investigates the impact of disabling PyTorch optimizations—specifically Automated Mixed Precision (AMP) and lower-precision matrix multiplication—on training a GPT-2 small base model from scratch. They modify the training code to optionally turn off AMP, addressing issues like non-finite gradients by manually scanning parameters to skip optimizer steps when needed. To maintain consistent batch size, they use an 8x A100 80 GiB machine with more VRAM, automating instance acquisition due to scarcity. The goal is to test if these optimizations affect model quality without compromising training stability.
标签: AI, LLM, PyTorch, training, optimization, AMP, gradients, hardware, code, experiment
